vision_profile.current_state: You are a beginner with foundational interest in Python, looking to secure your first internship in AI/ML.
vision_profile.main_blocker: Your biggest obstacle is translating theoretical knowledge into practical, industry-ready projects and understanding the full development lifecycle.
vision_profile.top_motivation: You are motivated to gain hands-on experience, build a strong portfolio, and launch a successful career in artificial intelligence.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: You can write clean Python scripts, manage code with Git, and understand basic data structures.
milestones.identity_statement: You are laying the essential groundwork, building the core skills that will define your journey as a Python developer.

project.title: Automated File Organizer
project.description: Develop a Python script that organizes files in a specified directory based on their type (e.g., images to 'Images' folder, documents to 'Documents' folder). The script should handle different file extensions and create new directories if they don't exist. Use Git for version control.
project.deliverable: A Python script (`file_organizer.py`) and a Git repository with commit history demonstrating the development process.
seeded_errors: The script incorrectly moves '.txt' files to the 'Documents' folder instead of 'TextFiles'.
The script fails to handle files with no extension, causing an error.

modules.title: Python for AI
lessons: Data Structures: Lists, Tuples, Dictionaries, Sets

video.title: Introduction to Python Programming
Mastering Python's Built-in Data Structures
Organizing Code with Functions and Modules

science.desc: A Python script for data processing is throwing a 'TypeError'. Identify the root cause and propose a fix.

video.title: Understanding Core Algorithmic Concepts
Analyzing Algorithm Efficiency with Big O
Implementing Common Searching and Sorting Algorithms

modules.title: Collaboration & Tools
lessons: Basic Git Commands (add, commit, push, pull)

video.title: Getting Started with Git and GitHub
Collaborating on Projects with GitHub
Effective Code Reviews for Quality Assurance

science.desc: Explain the difference between 'git merge' and 'git rebase' and when you would use each.

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: You can effectively clean, transform, and analyze data using Python libraries, and apply fundamental statistical concepts.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming adept at preparing data for analysis, a crucial step in any data-driven role.

project.title: Customer Churn Data Analysis
project.description: Perform an exploratory data analysis (EDA) on a customer churn dataset. Clean the data, handle missing values, identify outliers, and visualize key relationships between features and churn. Summarize your findings and suggest potential factors contributing to churn.
project.deliverable: A Jupyter Notebook (`churn_eda.ipynb`) with code, visualizations, and a markdown summary of findings.
seeded_errors: The notebook uses an inappropriate visualization type (e.g., scatter plot for categorical data) that misrepresents the data.
Missing values are imputed with a constant value without justification, potentially skewing results.

modules.title: Data Wrangling
lessons: Introduction to NumPy for Numerical Computing

video.title: Efficient Numerical Computing with NumPy
Manipulating Tabular Data with Pandas
Techniques for Robust Data Cleaning

science.desc: You're given a dataset with inconsistent date formats and duplicate entries. Describe your strategy to clean and standardize this data using Pandas.

modules.title: Math & Stats Essentials
lessons: Descriptive Statistics: Mean, Median, Mode, Variance
Introduction to Linear Algebra for ML

video.title: Summarizing Data with Descriptive Statistics
Foundations of Probability and Common Distributions
Drawing Conclusions with Hypothesis Testing

science.desc: Explain the Central Limit Theorem and its importance in statistical inference.

milestones.label: M03
milestones.o: You can build and evaluate basic supervised machine learning models, understanding feature engineering and model pipelines.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now building your first predictive models, seeing how data translates into actionable insights.

project.title: Predictive Model for Housing Prices
project.description: Build a supervised regression model to predict housing prices using a given dataset. Apply appropriate feature engineering techniques, train at least two different models (e.g., Linear Regression, Decision Tree), evaluate their performance, and select the best one. Document your process and findings.
project.deliverable: A Jupyter Notebook (`housing_price_predictor.ipynb`) with data preprocessing, model training, evaluation, and conclusions.
seeded_errors: The model is evaluated using R-squared on the training set only, leading to an overoptimistic performance estimate.
Categorical features are one-hot encoded without handling potential multicollinearity.

modules.title: Supervised Learning
lessons: Model Evaluation Metrics (Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1, MSE, R2)

video.title: Predicting Continuous Values with Linear Regression
Classifying Data with Logistic Regression

scenario.title: Interpreting a Misclassified Logistic Regression Model

video.title: Choosing the Right Metrics for Your ML Model

science.desc: A classification model has high accuracy but low recall for the positive class. What could be the problem and how would you address it?

modules.title: Feature Engineering & Pipelines
lessons: Creating New Features from Existing Ones

video.title: Crafting Effective Features for Machine Learning
Preparing Features: Scaling and Encoding Techniques
Streamlining ML Workflows with Pipelines

science.desc: Discuss the advantages of using scikit-learn pipelines in a machine learning project.

milestones.label: M04
milestones.o: You can design and implement robust, maintainable Python applications using Object-Oriented Programming principles.
milestones.identity_statement: You are transforming from a script writer to a software designer, building scalable and organized codebases.

project.title: Object-Oriented Data Processor
project.description: Refactor a procedural data processing script into an object-oriented Python application. Design classes for data loading, cleaning, and transformation, adhering to SOLID principles. The application should be modular and easily extensible for new processing steps.
project.deliverable: A Python package with well-defined classes and methods, along with a main script demonstrating its usage.
seeded_errors: The 'DataCleaner' class violates the Single Responsibility Principle by also handling data visualization.
A base 'Processor' class has a method that forces all subclasses to implement a specific data source, violating the Liskov Substitution Principle.

modules.title: OOP & SOLID Principles
lessons: SOLID Principles: Interface Segregation Principle (ISP) & Dependency Inversion Principle (DIP)

video.title: Building Modular Applications with Python OOP
Designing Maintainable Code with SOLID
Solving Common Problems with Design Patterns

science.desc: You're reviewing a class that handles both user authentication and database operations. How would you refactor this to adhere to the Single Responsibility Principle?

milestones.label: M05
milestones.o: You understand the core concepts of neural networks and can implement basic deep learning models using popular frameworks.
milestones.identity_statement: You are venturing into the powerful world of deep learning, unlocking new possibilities for complex problem-solving.

project.title: Image Classifier for MNIST Digits
project.description: Build and train a simple Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) using TensorFlow/Keras to classify handwritten digits from the MNIST dataset. Experiment with different architectures (number of layers, filter sizes) and activation functions. Evaluate the model's performance and visualize its predictions.
project.deliverable: A Jupyter Notebook (`mnist_cnn_classifier.ipynb`) demonstrating model construction, training, evaluation, and visualization.
seeded_errors: The model uses a 'relu' activation function in the output layer for a multi-class classification problem, leading to incorrect probability distributions.
The training loop does not include a validation set, making it difficult to detect overfitting early.

video.title: Demystifying the Basics of Neural Networks
Building Deep Learning Models with Keras

scenario.title: Debugging a Keras Model Training Issue

video.title: Introduction to CNNs for Image Processing

science.desc: Explain the role of activation functions in a neural network and name a few common ones.

milestones.label: M06
milestones.o: You can wrap machine learning models in APIs and deploy them, making them accessible for real-world applications.
milestones.identity_statement: You are bridging the gap between model development and real-world impact, making your creations accessible to others.

project.title: Simple ML Model API with FastAPI
project.description: Develop a FastAPI application that exposes a previously trained machine learning model (e.g., the housing price predictor or MNIST classifier) via a REST API. The API should accept input data, make predictions, and return the results. Containerize the application using Docker and deploy it locally.
project.deliverable: A FastAPI application (Python files), a trained model file, a `requirements.txt`, and a `Dockerfile`.
seeded_errors: The FastAPI endpoint expects a JSON payload but the model expects a NumPy array, causing a data type mismatch error.
The Dockerfile is missing a crucial dependency, preventing the application from starting inside the container.

video.title: Building High-Performance APIs with FastAPI
Ensuring Data Integrity with Pydantic
Exposing Machine Learning Models via REST APIs

science.desc: A FastAPI endpoint for a sentiment analysis model is receiving malformed JSON. How would you use Pydantic to validate the input and return a clear error message?

modules.title: Cloud & Deployment
lessons: Building Docker Images for Python Apps

video.title: Packaging Applications with Docker
Introduction to Deploying Applications in the Cloud
Keeping an Eye on Deployed Models

science.desc: What are the benefits of containerizing an application with Docker before deployment?

milestones.label: M07
milestones.o: You can write efficient and concurrent Python code, optimizing applications for better performance and responsiveness.
milestones.identity_statement: You are mastering the art of high-performance Python, ensuring your applications run smoothly and efficiently.

project.title: Concurrent Web Scraper
project.description: Develop a Python web scraper that fetches data from multiple URLs concurrently. Implement both threading and asyncio versions of the scraper to compare their performance. The scraper should handle potential network delays and errors gracefully.
project.deliverable: Two Python scripts (one using threading, one using asyncio) and a report comparing their performance and suitability for different scenarios.
seeded_errors: The threading-based scraper experiences a Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) bottleneck, negating performance gains for CPU-bound tasks.
The asyncio scraper does not properly await all coroutines, leading to incomplete data fetching.

video.title: Understanding Concurrency and Parallelism in Python
Leveraging Multithreading and Understanding the GIL

scenario.title: Optimizing an I/O Bound Task with Threads

video.title: Building Non-Blocking Applications with Asyncio

science.desc: When would you choose multiprocessing over multithreading in Python, and why?
